skill.author: zach22-1999

security_audit.summary: This skill is an Amazon product research and market analysis tool using Sorftime MCP data. It orchestrates multi-agent workflows (data-pipeline, insight-writer, report-writer) to deliver Go/No-Go product selection decisions. The vast majority of static scanner findings (536+ 'shell backtick execution' hits) are false positives — the backticks are inside markdown documentation examples, not actual code execution. The skill is documentation/prompt-orchestration heavy with supporting Python render scripts. No evidence of data exfiltration, prompt injection, or malicious intent was found.

medium_findings.title: PowerShell and shell command execution documented in usage guides
medium_findings.description: scripts/WINDOWS_USAGE.md contains PowerShell invocation examples (lines 42-43) and shell command substitution in scripts/parse_top100_dimensions.py:41. These are part of legitimate Python render pipeline documentation, but they represent external command execution patterns in the skill's supporting scripts.

medium_findings.title: env_access: Configuration library usage in cross_analysis.py
medium_findings.description: scripts/cross_analysis.py:65 accesses configuration library patterns. This is standard Python config handling, not credential exfiltration.

low_findings.title: False positive: backtick characters in markdown documentation
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 500+ instances of 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' across SKILL.md, README.md, agents/*.md, references/*.md. These are markdown inline code formatting characters (single backticks for code spans) in Chinese-language product research documentation, not actual shell execution. Verified as false positive after reviewing context — all are within markdown text or code examples shown to the user.

low_findings.title: False positive: system reconnaissance keywords in skill documentation
low_findings.description: Scanner flagged 'system reconnaissance' keywords in assets/dashboard_template.html and SKILL.md. These are CSS/HTML standard browser API references (navigator.userAgent, window.location, etc.) in the dashboard template, not actual reconnaissance behavior. Standard for a web dashboard rendering product analytics.

low_findings.title: False positive: high entropy in sample payload JSON
low_findings.description: evals/files/sample_payload_minimal.json shows high entropy strings — these are sample test data for the evaluation harness, not actual encoded payloads. The file is explicitly labeled as a minimal payload sample for evals.

dangerous_patterns.title: Weak cryptographic algorithm references in documentation
dangerous_patterns.description: Scanner flagged 'weak cryptographic algorithm' in SKILL.md:3, SKILL.md:1054, SKILL.md:1082, and references/payload_schema_v2.md. These are documentation references to algorithms like MD5/SHA1 in the context of Amazon ASIN identifiers and data hashing for product research — not used for security-critical operations. Acceptable for non-security hashing purposes.

dangerous_patterns.title: innerHTML usage in dashboard template
dangerous_patterns.description: assets/dashboard_template.html uses innerHTML assignments (18 locations) for rendering product research charts and tables. Since this is a static HTML template that receives data from the AI skill's output (not direct user input), the XSS risk is contained to the rendering context. Still flagged as a best-practice concern — textContent would be safer.

content.user_title: Research Amazon Products with Data-Driven Go/No-Go Decisions
content.value_statement: Selecting the wrong product wastes time and money. This skill uses Sorftime MCP to scan Amazon markets, analyze competition, and generate Go/No-Go reports so sellers can launch with confidence.
actual_capabilities: Scan Amazon categories and niches using Sorftime MCP data feeds
Tag and score products across multiple market dimensions (price, demand, competition, margin)
Generate cross-dimensional analysis comparing candidate products side by side
Validate competitive landscape including review count, BSR trends, and seller concentration
Calculate input/output ratios including cost, fees, and projected ROI
Produce a structured Go/No-Go report with HTML dashboard and actionable recommendations
limitations: Requires a working Sorftime MCP server connection and valid API credentials
Market data is limited to Amazon marketplaces and does not cover other e-commerce platforms
Cannot access real-time competitor pricing or inventory data outside of Sorftime's refresh cycle
Analysis quality depends on the product category depth in Sorftime's database

use_cases.title: New product sourcing for FBA sellers
use_cases.description: Amazon FBA sellers use this skill to evaluate candidate products before purchasing inventory, reducing risk of dead stock.
use_cases.target_user: Amazon FBA sellers and private label product researchers

use_cases.title: Market opportunity scanning for brand owners
use_cases.description: Brand owners scan entire categories to identify underserved niches and emerging product opportunities for brand launches.

use_cases.title: Competitive teardown for category managers
use_cases.description: Category managers use the cross-analysis output to understand competitor pricing, review velocity, and market share before entering or expanding a category.
use_cases.target_user: E-commerce category managers and market analysts

prompt_templates.title: Quick product scan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use /zach-product-research to scan the 'pet supplements' category on Amazon US and return the top 10 products with BSR, review count, and estimated margin.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-pass market scan for a new category

prompt_templates.title: Go/No-Go evaluation for a specific ASIN
prompt_templates.prompt: Run product research on ASIN B0EXAMPLE123. Include competition analysis, demand signals, and a final Go/No-Go recommendation with supporting data.
prompt_templates.scenario: Deep-dive on a single product candidate

prompt_templates.title: Cross-dimensional comparison
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare these 5 candidate products across price, demand, competition, and margin dimensions. Output a ranked recommendation table.

prompt_templates.title: Full market entry workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Start a full product research workflow for a new yoga mat brand. From market scan to final Go/No-Go report with HTML dashboard output.

output_examples.input: Scan Amazon US 'yoga mats' category and return top candidates with Go/No-Go assessment.
output: Top 3 candidate products identified with BSR, review count, and margin estimates
Competition density score for each candidate (1-10 scale)
Go/No-Go recommendation matrix with risk factors highlighted
HTML dashboard link with visual charts of market dimensions

output_examples.input: Deep-dive analysis on a specific ASIN with full competitive teardown.
output: Product overview with price history and BSR trend
Estimated ROI calculation including FBA fees and sourcing cost
Final Go/No-Go verdict with confidence score and key risks

output_examples.input: Compare 5 candidate products for private label launch.
output: Ranked comparison table across 6 market dimensions
Weighted scoring matrix with adjustable weights
Sensitivity analysis showing which assumptions matter most
Recommended next steps for the top candidate

best_practices: Always start with a category scan before drilling into individual products to understand market context
Use the cross-analysis view to compare at least 3 candidates side by side for balanced decision-making
Review the HTML dashboard output for visual trend analysis rather than relying on numbers alone
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill to research products without a working Sorftime MCP connection — results will be incomplete
Avoid making sourcing decisions based on a single dimension like BSR — always review the full multi-dimensional analysis
Do not skip the competitive teardown step when evaluating branded or trademarked product categories

faq.question: What data source does this skill use?
faq.answer: This skill uses the Sorftime MCP server to access Amazon market data including BSR, pricing, reviews, and seller metrics.

faq.question: Does it work for all Amazon marketplaces?
faq.answer: Yes, it supports all Amazon marketplaces available through your Sorftime MCP subscription, including US, EU, and Japan.

faq.question: What does the Go/No-Go report include?
faq.answer: The report includes product scoring across multiple dimensions, competitive analysis, margin estimates, risk factors, and a final recommendation with confidence level.

faq.question: Can I customize the scoring weights?
faq.answer: Yes, the cross-analysis step allows you to adjust dimension weights to match your sourcing criteria and risk tolerance.

faq.question: How long does a full analysis take?
faq.answer: A full category scan with deep-dive on 3-5 products typically completes in 5-10 minutes depending on data volume.

faq.question: Is Sorftime MCP a paid service?
faq.answer: Sorftime MCP requires a Sorftime subscription. This skill does not include or bundle Sorftime data — you must provide your own credentials.
